Figure 2:Linear kernelKLK_{L}eigenspectrum for MedSigLIP-448 atq=6q=6.
The kernel has exactly 6 positive eigenvalues (effective rank 5.53)
out ofN=1,896N=1{,}896training samples, which confirms that PCA-6 compression
collapses the kernel matrix to a 6-dimensional subspace.

Figure 3:Quantum vs. linear kernel eigenspectrum comparison for
MedSigLIP-448 atq∈{4,6}q\in\{4,6\}(left: normalized eigenvalue decay;
right: cumulative energy).
The quantum kernelKQK_{Q}has 1,133 (q=4) and 1,586 (q=6) eigenvalues
exceeding numerical thresholdε=10−10\varepsilon\!=\!10^{-10}; the theoretical
upper bound for the fidelity kernel is4q4^{q}(256 and 4,096 forq=4,6q=4,6),
so the surplus above that bound consists of finite-precision numerical
artefacts from statevector simulation.
For comparison, the linear kernel has exactly 4 and 6 algebraically
positive eigenvalues.
The red vertical line marks the rank boundary of the linear kernel
(Nλ>0=qN_{\lambda>0}=q), beyond which all linear kernel eigenvalues
collapse to numerical zero; the quantum kernel eigenspectrum extends
far beyond this boundary, a direct consequence of its higher effective rank.
Shannon effective rank (seed 0): quantumq=4q=4: 6.86 (1.82×1.82\timeslinear 3.77);
quantumq=6q=6: 13.94 (2.52×2.52\timeslinear 5.53).
The ratiogrowswith qubit count, consistent with the quantum
feature map accessing an exponentially larger Hilbert space (2q2^{q}dims).

Figure 4:Quantum kernel matrixKQK_{Q}(trace-normalized) for MedSigLIP-448
atq=6q=6(200 training samples sorted by class label). The off-diagonal
block structure reflects class boundaries; the quantum
feature map preserves discriminative signal in the same PCA subspace
where the linear kernel collapses (Table6).

Figure 5:Partial qubit sweep (q∈{2,3,4,5,6,8}q\in\{2,3,4,5,6,8\}, C = 1, DT9, seed_0):QSVMtest accuracy (left) and minority-class F1 (right) for all three models.
MedSigLIP-448 reaches F1 = 0.554 atq=8q=8; RAD-DINO and ViT-p32 improve
monotonically across this range with ViT collapsing atq=3q=3(F1 = 0).
Full results atq∈{9,10,11,12,16}q\in\{9,10,11,12,16\}are reported in Table2.

Figure 6:Quantum kernel eigenvalue spectra for all three embedding models
atq=4q=4(left) andq=6q=6(right).
MedSigLIP-448 (top), RAD-DINO (middle), ViT-patch32-cls (bottom).
The quantum kernel consistently exhibits higher effective rank than
the linear kernel across all models and qubit counts.
Figure 7:Quantum kernel eigenspectrum for MedSigLIP-448 at the performance peakq=11q=11(seed 0). Shannon effective rank = 43.04, a6.3×6.3\timesincrease fromq=4q=4(6.86); multi-seed mean is 69.80.
Figure 7:Quantum kernel eigenspectrum for MedSigLIP-448 at the performance peakq=11q=11(seed 0). Shannon effective rank = 43.04, a6.3×6.3\timesincrease fromq=4q=4(6.86); multi-seed mean is 69.80.

Figure 8:Quantum kernel heatmap for MedSigLIP-448 atq=11q=11, seed 0 (200 training samples). Compare withq=6q=6(Figure9): theq=11q=11kernel shows richer off-diagonal structure, consistent with its higher effective rank (43.04 vs. 13.94, seed 0).

Figure 9:Quantum kernel heatmaps for all three embedding models
atq=4q=4(left) andq=6q=6(right).
The block structure reflects class boundaries in the training data (sorted by label).
Higher qubit counts show sharper off-diagonal structure, consistent
with increased effective rank.

Figure 10:PCA feature space (q=4q=4, left;q=6q=6, right) for all three models
(MedSigLIP-448, RAD-DINO, ViT-patch32-cls, top to bottom).
Each point is a training sample projected onto its first two PCA components after
StandardScaler→\toPCA→\toMinMaxScaler preprocessing (seed 0).
Blue: Medicaid/Medicare; orange: Private insurance.
The two classes overlap substantially in every panel, which explains why the linear
kernelKLK_{L}—operating in this sameqq-dimensional subspace—collapses to
majority-class prediction (F1 = 0).
The label in the lower-left corner of each panel confirms classical SVM collapse at
that configuration.

Figure 11:PCA scatter of MedSigLIP-448 embeddings projected to 2 components
(total explained variance: 21.8%). Train set: 1319 majority (Medicare)
vs 577 minority (Private). The low explained variance confirms that the
2D PCA projection captures only a fraction of the structure exploited
by the quantum kernel in higher dimensions.
